channel.title: Home on Field Notes
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Field Notes

item.title: The Frame
item.description:
Aren’t we lucky?

We live in a time where we’re constantly connected. We text. We follow each other on social media. We send pictures back and forth throughout the day. We react, laugh, make plans, and then the conversation keeps moving. The goofy selfie that made me miss your face. A margarita glowing against a San Antonio sunset that somehow turns into plans for SeaWorld. A picture of Figs and Loki doing something ridiculous. A random chicken sandwich that looked particularly excellent.

The conversation continues, and those little moments are carried away by the current. Knowing myself, I probably won’t ever scroll back far enough to see them again.

So I bought a shared digital picture frame.

It sits on the desk in my foyer, a place I pass several times a day. Every time I walk by, it’s quietly showing me little glimpses of the people I care about.

I love it.

I can’t tell you how happy it makes me to see Figs and Loki, Jayden, Gary, Kobe, Theodore, and all the little moments that have already started collecting there. Maybe this is the part where I sound like a fogey, but it still amazes me that I can simply receive these tiny windows into your lives.

The best part is that they don’t have to be extraordinary. The extraordinary part is that you chose to share them. Every photo reminds me that, for a brief moment, you thought of me. That even though we weren’t together, you found a way to bring me into your day.

That’s the magic.

I don’t expect the Frame to preserve history. That’s what journals, photographs, and stories are for. I wanted something much simpler. I wanted a place where the people I care about could quietly continue showing up in my day. A little window that reminds me what you’ve been seeing lately.

This frame isn’t precious.

I expect blurry concert photos, terrible selfies, your pets, half-eaten lunches, random chicken sandwiches, rainy mornings, vacations, projects you’re proud of, and all the wonderfully ordinary moments that make up a life. If something catches your eye and makes you think, “Spyke would appreciate this,” I’d love to see it too.

Because if it’s special to you, it’s special to me.

If you’re part of my life and would like to leave the occasional sight on the Frame, mention it at the Front Desk and I’ll happily send you an invitation.

I just really miss your face.

item.title: Terminal Ritual
item.description: Small remote magic tonight. Is this digital scrying? Anyway here’s a photo of the sun from my window.

item.title: Sunday Morning
item.description:
Hydra engaged. Gave it a snack and then it wouldn’t stop following me. This is a terribly coded attempt at metaphor for I logged into Disney+ at home (subscription accidentally renewed, not mine, but might as well use it). Well… when in rome… I searched for Disney Classics and got Scrubs, Designing Women, Bewitched (a good one). Guess I’ll be keeping an eye out for DVDs. I just can’t with 20 tags for your whole library. Anyway where is filtering? Disney’s Junk Drawer.

Anyway, the demon and I posted a reel. But he’s bound to the Darkroom because he gets into everything and I’m bound to him. We’ll make the best of it.

item.title: First Sighting
item.description: Perched at the dais the beams find me. Dust motes come to my rescue flying between me and discovery. I’ve just got one more thing to do here.
